emitte

Retired statement-level frame emit; use expression ad with sermo directional views.

Syntax: emitte

Category

endpoint

Related

  • [ad](/corpus/ad.html)
  • [meus](/corpus/meus.html)
  • [tuus](/corpus/tuus.html)
  • [sermo](/corpus/sermo.html)

Examples

examples/corpus/emitte/emitte.fab (canonical · keyword)

Retired statement-level frame emit; use expression ad with sermo directional views.

# emitte — retired statement-level frame emit
#
# `emitte` is still a reserved lexer keyword so the parser can reject old stream
# blocks with a targeted diagnostic.
#
# GRAMMAR:
#   retiredEmitteStmt :← 'emitte' expr
#
# EXPECTED ERROR:
#   Statement-level `emitte` is retired. Use expression `ad` and a `sermo`
#   directional view such as `s.meus<T>().da(value)` or `s.tuus<T>().accipe()`.
#
# Retired form, intentionally commented out:
#   emitte scrinium { status = "item", data = "payload" }

incipit {
    nota "emitte is a retired statement form; use sermo directional views"
}

Expected output:

emitte is a retired statement form; use sermo directional views
Sources
  • ["examples/corpus/emitte/emitte.fab"]
